abstract.t: This document defines the "Payment" HTTP authentication scheme, enabling HTTP resources to require a payment challenge to be fulfilled before access. The scheme extends HTTP Authentication, using the HTTP 402 "Payment Required" status code.

The protocol is payment-method agnostic, supporting any payment network or currency through registered payment method identifiers. Specific payment methods are defined in separate payment method specifications.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: HTTP 402 "Payment Required" was reserved in HTTP/1.1 for future use but never standardized. This specification defines the "Payment" authentication scheme that gives 402 its semantics, enabling resources to require a payment challenge to be fulfilled before access.

section.name: Relationship to Payment Method Specifications
section.t: This specification defines the abstract protocol framework. Concrete payment methods are defined in payment method specifications that:

list.t: Register a payment method identifier
Define the request schema for that method
Define the payload schema for that method
Specify verification and settlement procedures

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dd.t: A WWW-Authenticate header with scheme "Payment" indicating the payment requirements for accessing a resource.
An Authorization header with scheme "Payment" containing payment authorization data.
A mechanism for transferring value, identified by a registered identifier.
The type of payment request, identified by a registered value in the IANA "HTTP Payment Intents" registry. Intents are defined by separate intent specifications.
Method-specific data in the challenge enabling payment completion. Encoded as base64url JSON in the request parameter.
Method-specific data in the credential proving payment.

figure.artwork: Client Server | | | (1) GET /resource | |------------------------------------------------>| | | | (2) 402 Payment Required | | WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="..", | | method="..", intent="..", request=".." | |<------------------------------------------------| | | | (3) Client fulfills payment challenge | | (signs transaction, pays invoice, etc.) | | | | (4) GET /resource | | Authorization: Payment <credential> | |------------------------------------------------>| | | | (5) Server verifies and settles | | | | (6) 200 OK | | Payment-Receipt: <receipt> | |<------------------------------------------------| | |

section.name: Status Codes

texttable.c: Valid format but payment verification failed

section.t: Servers MUST return 402 with a WWW-Authenticate: Payment header when payment is required. Servers SHOULD NOT return 402 without this header.

section.name: Relationship to 401 Unauthorized
section.t: This specification uses 402 (Payment Required) for the initial payment challenge, diverging from the traditional 401 pattern used by other HTTP authentication schemes. This distinction is intentional:

list.t: 402 indicates the resource requires payment (economic barrier)
401 indicates authentication/authorization failure (credential barrier)

section.t: When a client submits an invalid Payment credential, servers MUST return 401 (Unauthorized) with a WWW-Authenticate: Payment header containing a fresh challenge.

section.name: Challenge (WWW-Authenticate)
section.t: The Payment challenge is sent in the WWW-Authenticate header per . The challenge uses the auth-param syntax defined in Section 2.1 of :

figure.sourcecode: challenge = "Payment" [ 1*SP auth-params ] auth-params = auth-param *( OWS "," OWS auth-param ) auth-param = token BWS "=" BWS ( token / quoted-string )

section.name: Required Parameters
section.t: id: Unique identifier for this payment challenge. Servers MUST generate a cryptographically random value with at least 128 bits of entropy for each challenge. Clients MUST include this value in the credential to correlate the response with the challenge. Servers MUST reject credentials with unknown, expired, or already-used id values.
realm: Protection space identifier per . Servers MUST include this parameter to define the scope of the payment requirement.
method: Payment method identifier (). MUST be a lowercase ASCII string.
intent: Payment intent type (). The value MUST be a registered entry in the IANA "HTTP Payment Intents" registry.

request: Base64url-encoded JSON containing payment-method-specific data needed to complete payment. Structure is defined by the payment method specification. Padding characters ("=") MUST NOT be included.

section.name: Optional Parameters
section.t: expires: Timestamp indicating when this challenge expires, formatted as an date-time string (e.g., "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z"). Servers SHOULD include this parameter. Clients MUST NOT submit credentials for expired challenges.
description: Human-readable description of the resource or payment purpose. This parameter is for display purposes only and MUST NOT be relied upon for payment verification (see ).
Unknown parameters MUST be ignored by clients.

section.name: Example Challenge

figure.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required Cache-Control: no-store WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="x7Tg2pLqR9mKvNwY3hBcZa", realm="api.example.com", method="example", intent="charge", expires="2025-01-15T12:05:00Z", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOiIxMDAwIiwiY3VycmVuY3kiOiJVU0QiLCJyZWNpcGllbnQiOiJhY2N0XzEyMyJ9"

section.t: Example decoded request:

section.name: Credentials (Authorization)
section.t: The Payment credential is sent in the Authorization header using the b64token syntax as defined in :

figure.sourcecode: credentials = "Payment" 1*SP b64token b64token = 1*( ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" / "." / "_" / "~" / "+" / "/" ) *"="

section.t: The b64token value is a base64url-encoded JSON object (without padding) containing:
The payload field contains the payment-method-specific data needed to complete the payment challenge. Payment method specifications define the exact structure.

section.name: Example Credential

figure.sourcecode: GET /api/data HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6Ing3VGcycExxUjltS3ZOd1kzaEJjWmEiLCJwYXlsb2FkIjp7InByb29mIjoiMHhhYmMxMjMuLi4ifX0

section.t: Decoded credential:

section.name: Payment-Receipt Header
section.t: Servers SHOULD include a Payment-Receipt header on successful responses:

figure.sourcecode: Payment-Receipt = b64token

section.t: The decoded JSON object contains:

texttable.c: Method-specific reference (tx hash, invoice id, etc.)

section.t: Payment method specifications MAY define additional fields for receipts.

section.name: Payment-Authorization Header
section.t: Servers MAY include a Payment-Authorization header on successful responses to indicate that a credential may be reused for subsequent requests:

figure.sourcecode: Payment-Authorization = "Payment" 1*SP b64token *( OWS "," OWS auth-param )

section.t: The credential portion is directly usable as an Authorization header value. The following parameters are appended:

texttable.c: RFC 3339 timestamp after which the authorization expires
Protection space scope (defaults to challenge realm)

figure.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: application/json Payment-Receipt: eyJzdGF0dXMiOiJzdWNjZXNzIiwibWV0aG9kIjoiZXhhbXBsZSIsInRpbWVzdGFtcCI6IjIwMjUtMDEtMTVUMTI6MDA6MDBaIn0 Payment-Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6Ing3VGcycExxUjltS3ZOd1kzaEJjWmEifQ, expires="2025-01-16T12:00:00Z"

section.t: Subsequent requests reuse the credential directly:

figure.sourcecode: GET /api/data HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6Ing3VGcycExxUjltS3ZOd1kzaEJjWmEifQ

section.t: The server MAY return a different token in Payment-Authorization than the original credential (e.g., an access token optimized for reuse).

section.name: Client Behavior
section.t: Clients that receive a Payment-Authorization header SHOULD:

list.t: Extract the credential portion (Payment <b64token>) for reuse
Cache it along with the expires timestamp and realm
Use the cached credential for subsequent requests to the same realm
Stop reusing the credential after the expires timestamp

section.name: Server Behavior
section.t: Servers that issue Payment-Authorization MUST:

list.t: Track which authorizations are valid and not yet expired
Accept the issued credential for requests within the authorized realm
Return 401 with a fresh challenge if the authorization has expired
NOT require re-payment for requests within the authorization window

section.name: Reusing Credentials
section.t: Payment credentials are generally single-use unless the server explicitly grants reuse via the Payment-Authorization header.
Clients MUST NOT reuse Payment credentials across different challenges unless the server has returned a Payment-Authorization header.

section.name: Method Identifier Format
section.t: Payment methods are identified by lowercase ASCII strings:

figure.sourcecode: payment-method-id = method-name [ ":" sub-method ] method-name = 1*ALPHA sub-method = 1*( ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" )

section.t: Method identifiers are case-sensitive and MUST be lowercase.
The optional sub-method component allows payment methods to specify variants, networks, or chains. Payment method specifications MUST define the semantics of their sub-methods.

section.name: Method Registry
section.t: Payment methods are registered in the HTTP Payment Methods registry (). Each registered method has an associated specification that defines the request and payload schemas.

section.name: Payment Intents
section.t: Payment intents describe the type of payment being requested.

figure.sourcecode: intent = 1*( ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" )

section.name: Intent Specifications
section.t: Payment intents are defined in separate intent specifications that:

list.t: Define the semantic meaning of the intent
Specify required and optional request fields
Specify payload requirements
Define verification and settlement semantics
Register the intent in the Payment Intent Registry ()

section.t: See the Payment Intent Registry for registered intents.

section.name: Intent Negotiation
section.t: If a server supports multiple intents, it MAY issue multiple challenges:

figure.sourcecode: WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="abc", realm="api.example.com", method="example", intent="charge", request="..." WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="def", realm="api.example.com", method="example", intent="authorize", request="..."

section.t: Clients choose which challenge to respond to. Clients that do not recognize an intent SHOULD treat the challenge as unsupported.

section.name: Error Response Format
section.t: Servers SHOULD return JSON error bodies with 402 responses:

section.name: Retry Behavior
section.t: Servers SHOULD use the Retry-After HTTP header to indicate when clients may retry:

figure.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required Retry-After: 60 WWW-Authenticate: Payment ...

section.name: Payment Method Specifications
section.t: Payment method specifications MUST define:

list.t: Method Identifier: Unique lowercase string
Request Schema: JSON structure for the request parameter
Payload Schema: JSON structure for credential payloads
Verification Procedure: How servers validate proofs
Settlement Procedure: How payment is finalized
Security Considerations: Method-specific threats and mitigations

section.name: Custom Parameters
section.t: Implementations MAY define additional parameters in challenges:

list.t: Parameters MUST use lowercase names
Unknown parameters MUST be ignored by clients

section.name: Size Considerations
section.t: Servers SHOULD keep challenges under 8KB. Clients MUST be able to handle challenges of at least 4KB. Servers MUST be able to handle credentials of at least 4KB.

section.name: Character Encoding
section.t: All string values use UTF-8 encoding :

list.t: The request and credential payloads are JSON
Payment method identifiers are restricted to ASCII lowercase
The realm parameter SHOULD use ASCII-only values per

section.name: Human-Readable Text
section.t: The description parameter may contain localized text. Servers SHOULD use the Accept-Language request header to determine the appropriate language.

section.name: Threat Model
section.t: This specification assumes:

list.t: Attackers can observe all network traffic
Attackers can inject, modify, or replay messages
Attackers may control malicious servers or clients

section.name: Transport Security
section.t: Implementations MUST use TLS 1.2 or later when transmitting Payment challenges and credentials. Payment credentials contain sensitive authorization data that could result in financial loss if intercepted.
Servers MUST NOT issue Payment challenges over unencrypted HTTP. Clients MUST NOT send Payment credentials over unencrypted HTTP.

section.name: Challenge Identifier Security
section.t: The challenge id parameter MUST be:

list.t: Unpredictable: Not guessable by clients or attackers
Unique: Never reused across challenges
Bound: Each id MUST be bound to the origin, realm, and request

section.t: Servers MUST reject credentials containing invalid, expired, or previously-used id values.

section.name: Replay Protection
section.t: Payment methods MUST define their own replay protection mechanisms (e.g., nonce consumption, preimage revelation, authorization expiry).

section.name: Amount Verification
section.t: Clients MUST verify before authorizing payment:

list.t: Requested amount is reasonable for the resource
Recipient/address is expected
Currency/asset is as expected
Validity window is appropriate

section.t: Clients MUST NOT rely on the description parameter for payment verification. Malicious servers could provide a misleading description while the actual request payload requests a different amount.

list.t: Servers MUST NOT require user accounts for payment
Payment methods SHOULD support pseudonymous payments where possible
Servers SHOULD NOT log Payment credentials in plaintext

section.name: Credential Storage
section.t: Implementations MUST treat Authorization: Payment headers and Payment-Receipt headers as sensitive data.

section.name: Caching
section.t: Servers SHOULD send Cache-Control: no-store with 402 responses. Responses with Payment-Receipt or Payment-Authorization headers SHOULD include Cache-Control: private.

section.name: Cross-Origin Considerations
section.t: Clients (particularly browser-based wallets) SHOULD:

list.t: Clearly display the origin requesting payment
Require explicit user confirmation before authorizing payments
Not automatically respond to Payment challenges

section.name: Denial of Service
section.t: Servers SHOULD implement rate limiting on challenges issued and credential verification attempts.

section.name: Authorization Reuse
section.t: When servers issue Payment-Authorization headers:

list.t: Servers SHOULD use short authorization windows
Clients MUST store cached credentials securely
Servers MUST be able to revoke authorizations before expiry

section.name: Authentication Scheme Registration
section.t: This document registers the "Payment" authentication scheme in the "Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) Authentication Scheme Registry" established by :

list.t: Authentication Scheme Name: Payment
Reference: This document,
Notes: Used with HTTP 402 status code for proof-of-payment flows

section.name: Header Field Registration
section.t: This document registers the following header fields:

section.name: Payment Method Registry
section.t: This document establishes the "HTTP Payment Methods" registry. This registry uses the "Specification Required" policy defined in .
Registration requests must include:

list.t: Method Identifier: Unique lowercase ASCII string
Description: Brief description of the payment method
Reference: Reference to the specification document
Contact: Contact information for the registrant

section.name: Payment Intent Registry
section.t: This document establishes the "HTTP Payment Intents" registry. This registry uses the "Specification Required" policy defined in .
Registration requests must include:

list.t: Intent Identifier: Unique lowercase ASCII string
Description: Brief description of the intent semantics
Reference: Reference to the specification document
Contact: Contact information for the registrant

section.t: The registry is initially empty. Intent specifications register their identifiers upon publication.

figure.sourcecode:
; HTTP Authentication Challenge (following RFC 7235 Section 2.1) payment-challenge = "Payment" [ 1*SP auth-params ] auth-params = auth-param *( OWS "," OWS auth-param ) auth-param = token BWS "=" BWS ( token / quoted-string )
; Required parameters: id, realm, method, intent, request ; Optional parameters: expires, description
; HTTP Authorization Credentials (following RFC 6750 b64token) payment-credentials = "Payment" 1*SP b64token
; Payment-Receipt header field value Payment-Receipt = b64token
; Payment-Authorization header field value Payment-Authorization = "Payment" 1*SP b64token *( OWS "," OWS auth-param )
; Payment method identifier payment-method-id = method-name [ ":" sub-method ] method-name = 1*ALPHA sub-method = 1*( ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" )
; Payment intent intent = 1*( ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" )

section.name: One-Time Charge
section.t: A client requests a resource, receives a payment challenge, fulfills the payment, and receives the resource with a receipt.

figure.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required Cache-Control: no-store WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="qB3wErTyU7iOpAsD9fGhJk", realm="api.example.com", method="invoice", intent="charge", expires="2025-01-15T12:05:00Z", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOiIxMDAwIiwiY3VycmVuY3kiOiJVU0QiLCJpbnZvaWNlIjoiaW52XzEyMzQ1In0" Content-Type: application/json
{"error": "payment_required", "message": "Payment required for access"}

section.t: Decoded request:

figure.sourcecode: GET /resource HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6InFCM3dFclR5VTdpT3BBc0Q5ZkdoSmsiLCJwYXlsb2FkIjp7InByZWltYWdlIjoiMHhhYmMxMjMuLi4ifX0

section.t: Decoded credential:

figure.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Cache-Control: private Payment-Receipt: eyJzdGF0dXMiOiJzdWNjZXNzIiwibWV0aG9kIjoiaW52b2ljZSIsInRpbWVzdGFtcCI6IjIwMjUtMDEtMTVUMTI6MDA6MDBaIiwicmVmZXJlbmNlIjoiaW52XzEyMzQ1In0 Content-Type: application/json
{"data": "..."}

section.name: Signed Authorization
section.t: A payment method using cryptographic signatures:

figure.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required Cache-Control: no-store WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="zL4xCvBnM6kJhGfD8sAaWe", realm="api.example.com", method="signed", intent="charge", expires="2025-01-15T12:05:00Z", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOiI1MDAwIiwiYXNzZXQiOiJVU0QiLCJyZWNpcGllbnQiOiIweDc0MmQzNUNjNjYzNEMwNTMyOTI1YTNiODQ0QmM5ZTc1OTVmOGZFMDAiLCJub25jZSI6IjB4MTIzNDU2Nzg5MCJ9"

section.t: Decoded request:

section.name: Payment with Reusable Authorization
section.t: Server issues an authorization for subsequent requests:

figure.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Cache-Control: private Payment-Receipt: eyJzdGF0dXMiOiJzdWNjZXNzIn0 Payment-Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6InFCM3dFclR5VTdpT3BBc0Q5ZkdoSmsiLCJ0eXBlIjoic2Vzc2lvbiJ9, expires="2025-01-15T13:00:00Z" Content-Type: application/json
{"data": "..."}
GET /other-resource HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6InFCM3dFclR5VTdpT3BBc0Q5ZkdoSmsiLCJ0eXBlIjoic2Vzc2lvbiJ9

section.name: Multiple Payment Options
section.t: Server offers multiple payment methods:

figure.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required Cache-Control: no-store WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="pT7yHnKmQ2wErXsZ5vCbNl", realm="api.example.com", method="invoice", intent="charge", request="..." WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="mF8uJkLpO3qRtYsA6wDcVb", realm="api.example.com", method="signed", intent="charge", request="..."

section.t: Client selects preferred method and responds accordingly.

section.name: Failed Payment Verification

figure.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized Cache-Control: no-store WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="aB1cDeF2gHiJ3kLmN4oPqR", realm="api.example.com", method="invoice", intent="charge", request="..." Content-Type: application/json
{"error": "payment_verification_failed", "message": "Invalid payment proof"}

section.t: Note the use of 401 (not 402) for failed verification, with a fresh challenge for retry.

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: TBD
